name: Confidentiality Advisory Group
description: Provides Section 251 support under the NHS Act 2006, allowing the common law duty of confidentiality to be set aside for specific medical purposes where consent is not practicable. Required when using identifiable patient data without explicit consent in England and Wales.
role: Section 251 approval for use of identifiable data without consent

name: Public Benefit and Privacy Panel - Tier 1
description: Standard-track approval for research data access in Scotland. Handles straightforward requests for de-identified or aggregate data where governance requirements are well-established.
role: Standard data access approval for Scottish health data

tiers.description: For standard applications using de-identified data with established governance pathways
tiers.criteria: De-identified data, standard linkage, established precedent

name: Public Benefit and Privacy Panel - Tier 2
description: Complex-track approval for research data access in Scotland. Required for applications involving sensitive data, novel linkages, or where additional scrutiny is needed.
role: Complex/sensitive data access approval for Scottish health data

tiers.description: For complex applications involving sensitive data, novel linkages, or requiring additional scrutiny
tiers.criteria: Sensitive data, novel linkage, identifiable data, no established precedent

name: Health Research Authority / Research Ethics Committees
description: The HRA protects the interests of participants in health and social care research across the UK. Research Ethics Committees review research proposals for ethical standards. Required for most studies involving NHS patients, their data, or NHS staff.
role: Research ethics approval and HRA approval for NHS-related research

name: Northern Ireland Trusted Research Environment
description: Northern Ireland's Trusted Research Environment providing secure access to health and social care data from the HSC system. Managed as part of Northern Ireland's health data infrastructure.
role: Secure data access infrastructure for Northern Ireland health data

name: SAIL Information Governance Review Panel
description: Independent panel that reviews all applications for access to SAIL Databank. Includes public representatives and governance experts. Assesses whether proposed data use is appropriate, proportionate, and in the public interest.
role: Data access approval for SAIL Databank

name: Genomics England Data Access Committee
description: Reviews and approves applications for access to Genomics England research data. Assesses scientific merit, data governance, and alignment with participant consent.
role: Data access approval for Genomics England research environment

name: CPRD Independent Scientific Advisory Committee
description: Reviews scientific protocols for studies requesting CPRD data. Assesses scientific merit, feasibility, and whether the data request is proportionate to the research question.
role: Scientific and governance review for CPRD data access
